clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Evaluate BP-lowering benefit of smartphone-enhanced HBPM versus HBPM alone in uncontrolled hypertension
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Clinical decision-making for digital health integration, hypertension management planning, technology selection
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Recommendation against smartphone-only HBPM enhancement + identify need for cointerventions

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for digital health vs HBPM comparison
Display RCT findings on smartphone-enhanced HBPM
Choose between cuffless device or traditional HBPM
Identify needed cointerventions for BP control

deviceType.validation: Must be: smartphone_enhanced_HBPM, HBPM_alone, cuffless_device, reference_device

validationRules: Cuffless device requires acknowledgment of heterogeneity risk

rules: If smartphone-enhanced HBPM vs HBPM alone in adults with uncontrolled BP → NO BP-lowering benefit
If no BP-lowering benefit detected → recommend cointerventions
If cuffless device vs reference device → calculate pooled mean difference
If SBP pooled mean difference = 3.42 mm Hg (95% CI: -2.17 to 9.01) → no statistically significant difference
If DBP pooled mean difference = 1.16 mm Hg (95% CI: -1.26 to 3.58) → no statistically significant difference
If cuffless device recommended → flag heterogeneity risk (underestimation/overestimation potential)
If patient is pregnant with chronic or gestational hypertension → flag need for additional research
If HBPM used without cointervention → recommend adding interventions for meaningful BP control

categories.name: Cuffless Devices
categories.recommendation: Cannot recommend for clinical use yet

recommendedNextStep.description: e.g., 'Add cointerventions to HBPM' or 'Wait for more research'

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Do not use smartphone-only HBPM without cointerventions; consider adding lifestyle or behavioral interventions
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Re-evaluate when new RCT evidence available
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor for cuffless device validation studies
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag pregnant patients as needing specialized HBPM protocols
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log device type, cointerventions, and evidence-based recommendation
